notes: EU AI governance frameworks including ALTAI self-assessment tool, Ethics Guidelines for Trustworthy AI, and the EU AI Act's control requirements (risk categories, prohibited practices).

data.description: Practical self-assessment tool for operationalizing the EU's Ethics Guidelines for Trustworthy AI, organized around seven key requirements.
data.notes: Published 2020 by the High-Level Expert Group on AI. Implements the 2019 Ethics Guidelines. Voluntary self-assessment tool. Over 100 assessment questions across seven requirements: human agency and oversight, technical robustness and safety, privacy and data governance, transparency, diversity/non-discrimination/fairness, societal and environmental well-being, accountability. Basis for AI Act compliance preparation. Online interactive tool available at altai.insight-centre.org.

versions_available.note: First edition. 100+ assessment questions across 7 key requirements.

data.description: Defines what makes AI systems trustworthy: lawful (complying with laws), ethical (respecting ethical principles), and robust (technically and socially reliable).
data.notes: Published April 2019 by the High-Level Expert Group on AI (52 experts). Foundation for ALTAI self-assessment tool and the EU AI Act. Widely referenced globally. Three pillars: lawful, ethical, robust. Establishes the seven key requirements that ALTAI operationalizes.

versions_available.note: First and only edition. Foundation document for EU AI governance.

data.description: The world's first comprehensive AI regulation, establishing a risk-based classification system with requirements for AI systems based on their risk level.
data.notes: Entered into force August 2024, phased implementation through 2027. Risk categories: prohibited (banned practices like social scoring, certain biometrics), high-risk (strict requirements, conformity assessment), limited-risk (transparency obligations), minimal-risk (no specific requirements). High-risk domains include biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, and justice. Key requirements for high-risk: risk management system, data governance, technical documentation, record-keeping, transparency, human oversight, accuracy/robustness/security. Significant penalties (up to 7% global revenue). Creates European AI Office.

versions_available.note: First and only version. Phased implementation through 2027.
